description: Establish a faithful host-based verification harness and capture a fully-green baseline BEFORE any code changes. The project's real toolchain (vitest, esbuild, eslint, @types) cannot be installed because the npm registry is network-blocked (INTEGRATIONS_ONLY, proxy 403). Build a node:test-based runner that executes the UNMODIFIED src/test/**/*.test.ts files against the real source, plus a bun build smoke check. This harness becomes the authoritative gate for verifying bug fixes in FEAT-002.
steps: Confirm the environment: run `env -u NODE_OPTIONS node --version` (expect v22.x), `env -u NODE_OPTIONS bun --version` (expect 1.2.x), and `command -v tsc eslint`. Remember the CRITICAL gotcha: NODE_OPTIONS points to a missing proxy-bootstrap.js, so EVERY node/npm/bun/tsc command must be prefixed with `env -u NODE_OPTIONS`. Do NOT attempt `npm ci`/`npm install` (it hangs on the blocked registry); do not rely on a node_modules directory.
Create a git-tracked .verify/ directory for all harness scaffolding. Add .verify/ output artifacts (e.g. .verify/last-run.txt) to .gitignore but keep the harness source files tracked.

Write .verify/vitest-shim.mjs: a faithful vitest-compatibility module implementing exports {describe, it, test, expect, beforeAll, beforeEach, afterAll, afterEach, vi} on top of node:test (node:test provides describe/it/test/before*/after*; build expect yourself). expect must support the matchers the suite uses: toBe, toEqual, toStrictEqual, toContain, toContainEqual, toThrow, toMatch, toBeCloseTo, toBeGreaterThan, toBeGreaterThanOrEqual, toBeLessThan, toBeLessThanOrEqual, toBeDefined, toBeUndefined, toBeNull, toBeNaN, toBeTruthy, toBeFalsy, toBeInstanceOf, toHaveLength, toHaveProperty, toHaveBeenCalled, toHaveBeenCalledTimes, toHaveBeenCalledWith, plus the `.not` negation chain and `.resolves`/`.rejects` async chains. Implement deep equality for toEqual/toContainEqual/toHaveBeenCalledWith (structural, like vitest). Make matcher failures throw with a clear message so node:test reports them as failures.

Implement `vi` in the shim: vi.fn(impl?) returning a callable mock that records .mock.calls and supports mockReturnValue/mockResolvedValue/mockImplementation and works with toHaveBeenCalled*; vi.spyOn(obj, method); vi.clearAllMocks/resetAllMocks/restoreAllMocks; vi.useFakeTimers/useRealTimers/advanceTimersByTime(/Async)/runAllTimers/runOnlyPendingTimers/setSystemTime implemented by swapping globalThis.setTimeout/clearTimeout/setInterval/clearInterval and Date.now with a controllable virtual clock (and restoring originals on useRealTimers); vi.mock(specifier, factory) and vi.unmock — register the factory in a global registry consumed by the resolve/load hook (step 5). Match vitest semantics closely enough that the existing tests pass unchanged.

Write .verify/loader.mjs: a node module-customization hook (registered via `node --import ./.verify/register.mjs` using module.register, or `node --loader`) that (a) resolves bare specifier 'vitest' to .verify/vitest-shim.mjs, (b) resolves 'vscode' to src/test/__mocks__/vscode.ts, and (c) honors vi.mock factories registered for a specifier (return synthetic module source from the registered factory) so that vi.mock('vscode', factory) / vi.mock('fs', factory) behave faithfully. Node 22 strips TS types natively, so .ts test+source files run directly; ensure the hook transpiles/strips .ts if needed (node 22.22 strips by default for .ts; verify).

Write a runner: a package.json script (e.g. "test:host") OR .verify/run.mjs that discovers all src/test/**/*.test.ts files and runs them with `env -u NODE_OPTIONS node --import ./.verify/register.mjs --test <files>` (or node --test with the glob). Ensure it exits non-zero if any test fails. Do NOT modify any existing test file or the real vitest.config.ts / tsconfig.json.

Run the full host harness over ALL 74 test files. Iterate on the shim/hook ONLY (never edit the tests) until the baseline is fully green — every test passes. Cross-check the total test count is in the same ballpark as `bun test` reported (~1210 cases) so no files are silently skipped. If a handful of tests rely on vitest behavior that is genuinely impractical to reproduce, document each one explicitly in findings (file + reason) rather than skipping silently — but aim for 100% green.

Add a build smoke check to the runner/docs: `env -u NODE_OPTIONS bun build src/extension.ts --target=node --external vscode --external onnxruntime-node --external @huggingface/transformers --outfile /tmp/voxpilot-bundle.js` and confirm exit 0 (faithful proxy for the esbuild bundle since esbuild is uninstallable).

Best-effort typecheck: attempt `env -u NODE_OPTIONS tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`. It will report many TS2307 'vscode' and TS2591 Buffer/process errors caused by missing @types packages (environmental, not bugs). Optionally add .verify/ambient.d.ts (declare module 'vscode'; minimal Node globals) referenced by a SEPARATE .verify/tsconfig.json so REAL type errors can surface — never weaken src or the root tsconfig.json. Record the typecheck situation in findings.

Record the baseline in this feature's findings: exact host-harness pass/fail counts, build smoke result, typecheck situation, and confirmation that NO source or test files were modified to achieve green. This is the 'prior pass' reference state.
acceptance_criteria: Running the host test harness (`env -u NODE_OPTIONS node --import ./.verify/register.mjs --test src/test/**/*.test.ts` or the documented runner script) executes all 74 unmodified test files and reports 0 failures.
The harness covers approximately the full ~1210 test cases (no test file is silently dropped); any unavoidable exclusions are explicitly documented in findings with reasons.
`env -u NODE_OPTIONS bun build src/extension.ts --target=node --external vscode --external onnxruntime-node --external @huggingface/transformers --outfile /tmp/voxpilot-bundle.js` exits 0.

No existing test file, vitest.config.ts, or tsconfig.json was modified; all harness code lives under .verify/. Working tree changes are limited to .verify/ (+ .gitignore + optional package.json script).
FEAT-001 findings document the exact baseline counts, build smoke result, and typecheck constraint.
verification: env -u NODE_OPTIONS <documented host test runner> ; confirm exit code 0 and 0 failing tests across all 74 files
env -u NODE_OPTIONS bun build src/extension.ts --target=node --external vscode --external onnxruntime-node --external @huggingface/transformers --outfile /tmp/voxpilot-bundle.js ; confirm exit 0
git status --short ; confirm only .verify/ (+ .gitignore/package.json script) changed, no test/src edits
findings: BASELINE VERIFIED (prior-pass harness re-validated by orchestrator). Environment: node v22.22.3, bun 1.2.14; NODE_OPTIONS gotcha confirmed (must prefix every node/bun/tsc invocation with `env -u NODE_OPTIONS`). Network INTEGRATIONS_ONLY so npm install/vitest/esbuild are unavailable; the .verify/ node:test harness is the authoritative gate. HOST HARNESS RESULT (`env -u NODE_OPTIONS node .verify/run.mjs`): files=74, tests=1320, pass=1320, fail=0, exit 0 — fully green with NO source/test files modified. BUILD SMOKE (`bun build src/extension.ts --target=node --external vscode --external onnxruntime-node --external @huggingface/transformers --outfile /tmp/voxpilot-bundle.js`): bundled 73 modules, 0.54MB, exit 0. Harness scaffolding lives entirely under .verify/ (vitest-shim.mjs, loader.mjs, register.mjs, run.mjs, mock-registry.mjs, ambient.d.ts, tsconfig.json); package.json adds a `test:host` script; .gitignore ignores .verify/last-run.txt and *.log. This is the 'prior pass' reference state for FEAT-002.
